problems.name: Problem 8 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: For how many three-digit whole numbers does the sum of the digits equal $25$?

problems.name: Problem 9 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: A shopper buys a $100$ dollar coat on sale for $20\%$ off. An additional $5$ dollars are taken off the sale price by using a discount coupon. A sales tax of $8\%$ is paid on the final selling price. The total amount the shopper pays for the coat is

problems.name: Problem 10 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: For how many positive integer values of $N$ is the expression $\frac{36}{N+2}$ an integer?

problems.name: Problem 11 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: Last summer $100$ students attended basketball camp. Of those attending, $52$ were boys and $48$ were girls. Also, $40$ students were from Jonas Middle School and $60$ were from Clay Middle School. Twenty of the girls were from Jonas Middle School. How many of the boys were from Clay Middle School?

problems.name: Problem 12 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: Each of the three large squares shown below is the same size. Segments that intersect the sides of the squares intersect at the midpoints of the sides. How do the shaded areas of these squares compare? [See figure in original PDF]
answerChoices: The shaded areas in all three are equal.
Only the shaded areas of I and II are equal.
Only the shaded areas of I and III are equal.
Only the shaded areas of II and III are equal.
The shaded areas of I, II and III are all different.

problems.name: Problem 16 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: The perimeter of one square is $3$ times the perimeter of another square. The area of the larger square is how many times the area of the smaller square?

problems.name: Problem 17 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: Pauline Bunyan can shovel snow at the rate of $20$ cubic yards for the first hour, $19$ cubic yards for the second, $18$ for the third, etc., always shoveling one cubic yard less per hour than the previous hour. If her driveway is $4$ yards wide, $10$ yards long, and covered with snow $3$ yards deep, then the number of hours it will take her to shovel it clean is closest to

problems.name: Problem 18 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: Mike leaves home and drives slowly east through city traffic. When he reaches the highway he drives east more rapidly until he reaches the shopping mall where he stops. He shops at the mall for an hour. Mike returns home by the same route as he came, driving west rapidly along the highway and then slowly through city traffic. Each graph shows the distance from home on the vertical axis versus the time elapsed since leaving home on the horizontal axis. Which graph is the best representation of Mike's trip? [See figure in original PDF]

problems.name: Problem 24 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: A $2$ by $2$ square is divided into four $1$ by $1$ squares. Each of the small squares is to be painted either green or red. In how many different ways can the painting be accomplished so that no green square shares its top or right side with any red square? There may be as few as zero or as many as four small green squares.

problems.name: Problem 25 (1994 AJHSME)
problems.statement: Find the sum of the digits in the answer to $\underbrace{999\dots99}_{94\text{ nines}} \times \underbrace{444\dots44}_{94\text{ fours}}$ where a string of $94$ nines is multiplied by a string of $94$ fours.
